entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan, the crop studied for dry matter accumulation under different moisture regimes

entities.name: WELL-WATERED
entities.description: A moisture regime where water is provided sufficiently to the crop

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A moisture regime where water is limited from flowering until maturity

entities.name: ICRISAT ASIA CENTER, INDIA
entities.description: The research facility where the pigeonpea genotypes were grown

entities.name: CUMULATIVE INTERCEPTED PHOTOSYNTHETICALLY ACTIVE RADIATION (CIR)
entities.description: The total amount of photosynthetically active radiation intercepted by the crop canopy over time

entities.name: BIOMASS ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The process of increasing total plant mass

entities.name: RADIATION USE EFFICIENCY (RUE)
entities.description: The efficiency with which intercepted radiation is converted into biomass

entities.name: CANOPY ATTRIBUTES
entities.description: Physical characteristics of the crop canopy that influence growth and radiation interception

entities.name: LEAF AREA DURATION
entities.description: The integral of leaf area over the growth period

entities.name: CROP GROWTH RATE (CGR)
entities.description: The rate of increase in crop biomass over time

entities.name: DRY MATTER ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The accumulation of organic matter in the plant excluding water content

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall output or yield of the pigeonpea crop

entities.name: RAIN-OUT SHELTERS
entities.description: Automated structures used to control and impose specific moisture regimes

relationships.description: affected the slope of the relationship
accounted for nearly 90% of genotypic variation
explained more than 70% of genotypic variation
